title: Bulenox Rules Explained: Drawdown, Profit Targets & Time Limits (2026)

excerpt: Every Bulenox rule explained in plain language — drawdown limits, profit targets, trading restrictions, and payout terms.
content:
Bulenox Rules Overview

Bulenox is a futures prop firm founded in 2022 and based in Wilmington, Delaware. The firm specializes in funded trading accounts ranging from $10,000 to $250,000 and operates under an evaluation-then-live model. Unlike many competitors, Bulenox offers a unique profit structure: traders retain 100% of profits on the first $10,000 earned, then switch to a 90/10 split (90% to trader, 10% to firm) beyond that threshold.

The firm caters specifically to futures day traders, algorithmic traders, and Expert Advisor (EA) users with support for 42 futures instruments including rare access to Micro Bitcoin (MBT). Bulenox requires NinjaTrader 8 as the exclusive trading platform and does not support alternative platforms like Tradovate or Rithmic.

Key differentiators include an unlimited evaluation phase with no time constraints, frequent promotional pricing (up to 75% off), and full automation support for algo and EA trading. However, the firm operates futures only—no stocks, forex, options, or CFDs.

Account Types and Pricing

Bulenox offers six account tiers with corresponding monthly subscription fees. All accounts are subject to recurring monthly charges until reaching the Master phase (funded status).

Account Size Monthly Fee Fee Per Trading Day (Approx.)
$10,000 $115 ~$5.75
$25,000 $143 ~$7.15
$50,000 $195 ~$9.75
$100,000 $295 ~$14.75
$150,000 $395 ~$19.75
$250,000 $535 ~$26.75

Important: Monthly fees are recurring charges that accumulate until you reach funded (Master) status. A trader on the $10K account paying $115/month for a 6-month evaluation incurs $690 in fees before accessing real capital. Bulenox frequently runs promotions offering up to 75% off these fees, significantly reducing entry costs for new traders.

Bulenox does not offer a free tier or demo account. Trial periods are not mentioned in standard offerings.

Profit Targets

Bulenox evaluations are unlimited in duration—there is no fixed time limit to reach profit targets. This is a significant advantage for traders who prefer steady, lower-risk trading over aggressive scaling.

Based on the firm's structure, the evaluation phase typically requires traders to demonstrate consistent profitability before advancing to funded status (Master phase). Specific profit target percentages are not published in standard marketing materials. Traders should contact Bulenox directly for exact profit thresholds for each account tier.

The profit target structure may vary by account size and market conditions. Since Bulenox does not enforce strict time limits, traders can trade at their own pace without pressure to hit targets quickly.

Drawdown Rules

Drawdown management is critical in prop trading. Specific daily and maximum drawdown limits for Bulenox are not fully detailed in publicly available materials. However, based on industry standards for prop firms, typical structures include:

  • Daily Drawdown Limit: A fixed dollar amount or percentage loss allowed per trading day
  • Maximum (Trailing) Drawdown: Total loss allowed from the account's highest equity point before account termination
  • Reset Conditions: Whether drawdowns reset daily or use a trailing calculation throughout the evaluation phase

Traders should review Bulenox's official trading agreement and risk policy documentation for exact drawdown limits, as these directly impact trading strategy and position sizing. The firm's unlimited evaluation phase suggests a more lenient approach to drawdown recovery—traders can rebuild equity over time without being rushed into funded status.

Trading Restrictions

Bulenox's trading rules reflect its focus on futures and algo trading:

  • Allowed Instruments: 42 futures contracts including equities, commodities, forex futures, crypto futures, and notably Micro Bitcoin (MBT), a rare offering among prop firms
  • Algorithmic Trading & EAs: Fully permitted—Bulenox explicitly allows automated trading strategies, a major selling point
  • Copy Trading: Status not publicly specified; contact the firm for clarification
  • Weekend Holding: Applies only to overnight positions in instruments with gaps (crypto, index futures). Position management depends on instrument liquidity
  • News Trading: Not explicitly restricted, but high-volatility trading during major economic announcements may be monitored
  • Lot Size Limits: Not specified in available data; likely scaled by account size and daily loss limits
  • Platform Requirement: NinjaTrader 8 only—no Tradovate, Rithmic, or other platform alternatives allowed
  • Time Limits: No fixed trading hours restriction; futures markets operate nearly 24/5

No Stock, Forex Spot, Options, or CFD Trading: Bulenox is strictly futures-focused. Traders cannot trade equities, spot forex, or derivatives through this firm.

Profit Split and Payouts

Bulenox's profit-sharing model is one of the most attractive in the prop trading industry:

  • First $10,000 Earned: 100% retained by the trader (the firm takes zero commission)
  • Beyond $10,000: 90/10 split—traders keep 90%, firm keeps 10%

This structure incentivizes traders to reach the $10K profit milestone quickly, as subsequent earnings flow to the trader at an industry-leading 90% rate.

Withdrawal Frequency: Specific withdrawal schedules are not detailed in standard materials. Most prop firms allow weekly or monthly withdrawals. Traders should confirm with Bulenox.

Minimum Withdrawal Amount: Not publicly specified. Typical prop firms require minimums of $100–$500 to process withdrawals.

Processing Time: Not specified. Standard processing typically takes 3–7 business days via ACH or wire transfer.

Payments are made only from profits earned during live trading on the funded account. Evaluation phase trading (if any) does not generate payouts.

Scaling Plan

Account scaling details for Bulenox are not extensively published. Industry-standard scaling models for prop firms typically follow this pattern:

  • Interim Milestones: Traders may qualify for temporary account increases (e.g., $15K to $25K) after hitting initial profit targets
  • Master Phase Scaling: Once reaching funded status, traders can access the full account size with trading rules aligned to their tier
  • Scaling Criteria: Consistent profitability, low drawdown, risk management compliance

Exact scaling timelines and requirements should be confirmed directly with Bulenox, as the firm's unlimited evaluation phase may allow flexible progression rather than rigid milestones.

Bulenox Rules vs. Competitors

To understand Bulenox's competitive positioning, here's a comparison with other top prop firms:

Feature Bulenox Competitor A Competitor B
Futures Only Yes Stocks + Forex Stocks + Futures
100% Profit First $10K Yes No (80/20 split) No (70/30 split)
EA Trading Allowed Restricted Allowed
Evaluation Time Limit Unlimited 60 days 90 days
Minimum Account Size $10,000 $5,000 $10,000
Monthly Fee (10K) $115 (or $29 with 75% promo) $99 $109
Crypto Futures Access Yes, including Micro Bitcoin Limited Yes

Bulenox Strengths: The 100% profit split on the first $10,000 is rare and valuable. Unlimited evaluation time removes pressure and allows sustainable trading practices. Full EA/algo support appeals to quant traders. Micro Bitcoin access is uncommon.

Bulenox Weaknesses: Futures-only limitation excludes stock traders. Newer firm (2022) versus established competitors with longer track records. Monthly fees still recur and can accumulate if evaluation is prolonged, unlike some competitors offering flat one-time costs.

Red Flags and Fine Print

Before joining Bulenox, traders should be aware of these considerations:

  • Recurring Monthly Fees: Fees continue every month until funded status is reached. A 6-month evaluation costs $690 on the $10K account—that's capital needed upfront. Promotions help, but full terms should be reviewed.
  • Firm Age and Track Record: Founded in 2022, Bulenox is newer than established competitors (5-15+ year histories). Research client testimonials and dispute resolution records.
  • NinjaTrader 8 Only: This is not a limitation for all traders, but it eliminates flexibility if you prefer alternative platforms. No migration option to other platforms mid-evaluation.
  • Futures Volatility: Futures trading is inherently leveraged and volatile. Daily stop-outs are possible, especially for aggressive strategies. The unlimited evaluation phase may be required because of this volatility.
  • Profit Target Opacity: Exact profit targets for reaching Master phase are not published. Contact the firm before funding your account to confirm requirements.
  • Withdrawal Terms: Specific minimum withdrawal amounts, processing times, and fee structures are not detailed. Clarify payment terms in writing.
  • Regulatory Status: Bulenox operates as a funded trading firm. Ensure you understand the legal status in your jurisdiction and any tax implications of funded account trading.
  • Marketing Claims: The 4.1/5 rating and "100% profit split" are compelling but should be verified independently. Request client success rates, not just advertised benefits.

Key Takeaways

Bulenox is best suited for futures day traders and algorithmic traders seeking a high profit-share structure with minimal time pressure. The 100% split on the first $10,000 and unlimited evaluation phase differentiate it from many competitors. However, the futures-only market, NinjaTrader 8 requirement, and recurring monthly fees may not appeal to all traders.

Interested traders should contact Bulenox directly to confirm exact drawdown limits, profit targets, scaling timelines, and withdrawal procedures before committing capital.

For more information, visit the Bulenox review page or compare with other prop firms on TradingToolsHub.
